RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 406-A, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Melissa Schmidt, a Republican, represents Wright County and parts of Laclede, Webster, and Douglas Counties (District 141) in the Missouri House of Representatives. She was elected to her first two-year term in November 2024.
Rep. Schmidt has a strong background in education and public service. She has spent much of her career in non-for-profit development and program coordination within the community. Additionally, she is a state certified childcare center director with over 30 years of experience working with children and their families in the community in public and private education.
Rep. Schmidt has previously served constituents in the role of Legislative Assistant within the House.
Outside of her legislative duties, Rep. Schmidt is deeply involved in her community. She holds leadership roles within her Cross Creek Church family and is an active member of both Kiwanis and Rotary clubs. She also serves on the Lebanon R-3 Early Childhood Advisory Board, helping to shape the future of early childhood education in her area.
Rep. Schmidt enjoys spending quality time with her husband, Aaron, and their two adult children. Together, they are committed to serving their community and making a positive impact on the lives of others.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions relating to the protection of children
Modifies provisions relating to licensure of child care facilities
Modifies provisions relating to health screenings for a child taken into the custody of the children's division
Establishes the "Zero-Cost Adoption Fund Act", which establishes a fund to offset costs associated with adoption and a tax credit for any person who contributes to the fund

Requires physicians prescribing abortion medication to provide information to the woman seeking the abortion about reversal medication and requires the posting of such information in several public areas

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions relating to human trafficking
Establishes provisions relating to civil liability for publishing or distributing material harmful to minors on the internet
Changes the law regarding firearm concealed carry permits
Creates provisions relating to prior authorization of health care services
Modifies provisions relating to the list of diseases required to be reported to the department of health and senior services
Modifies provisions relating to income tax credits for contributions to pregnancy resource centers, maternity homes, and diaper banks
Designates the last week in April each year as "Infertility Awareness Week"
Proposes a constitutional amendment relating to property tax exemptions
